title: Cordiera oligantha

uses: Cordiera oligantha is traditionally used in folk medicine in the Amazon region, particularly in Brazil and Peru. The plant is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. intro: Cordiera oligantha, also known as the Cordiera plant, is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The Cordiera plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which can aid in treating fever, malaria, and other infectious diseases. The plant contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cordiera oligantha is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, coughs, and digestive issues.
